hero.title:
Build your product
faster, with AI.
hero.subtitle: I work with AI tools throughout the entire build — faster prototyping, faster iteration, faster delivery. Available for projects, products, and long-term partnerships.

vibe.label: HOW I WORK
vibe.title: Vibe coding: from prompt to product
vibe.subtitle: I write the requirements, AI writes the code. I review, refine, and ship. Less time on boilerplate, more time solving the right problems.
vibe.point1: AI-assisted from day one — design, code, and debug with Claude
vibe.point2: Tight feedback loops — visible progress every day, not every sprint
vibe.point3: Production-ready code — real deployment, not just demos

products.sectionSubtitle: A selection of systems I've designed and built.

notecast.subtitle: Self-hosted personal note management system
notecast.description: A self-hosted, single-user note management system with block-based EditorJS editor, multi-platform publishing to WeChat and Feishu, AI-powered translation, and workspace management. This portfolio runs on it.

ims.subtitle: Construction site material inventory management
ims.description: Full-lifecycle inventory system for construction sites: procurement, multi-warehouse tracking, multi-level approval workflows, borrow/return management, and complete transaction audit trails.

trainadmin.subtitle: SaaS platform for dance training institutions
trainadmin.description: Multi-tenant SaaS platform for dance training institutions — class scheduling, student management, attendance tracking, payment processing, and instructor dashboards.

productBuild.title: Product Build
productBuild.description: From idea to deployed product. I build full-stack web systems fast, using AI tools throughout. Typical timeline: 2-8 weeks.

hero.title: What I can build for you
hero.subtitle: Concrete services from an AI-powered independent developer. I ship products fast, integrate AI into existing systems, and build the internal tools your team actually uses.

productBuild.title: Product Build
productBuild.desc: From idea to deployed product. I build full-stack web systems fast, using AI tools throughout the entire process. Typical timeline: 2-8 weeks. Real examples: TrainAdmin (SaaS), IMS (inventory system), Notecast (note system).

aiIntegration.title: AI Integration
aiIntegration.desc: Add AI capabilities to your existing system: intelligent search, document processing, chat assistants, automatic summarization, or workflow automation powered by Claude or OpenAI-compatible APIs.

businessSystems.title: Business Systems
businessSystems.desc: Internal tools and admin systems your team actually uses: approval workflows, inventory management, dashboards, CRM modules, and process automation. Built for real teams, not demos.

partnership.title: Technical Partnership
partnership.desc: Monthly engagement: ongoing development, bug fixes, maintenance, and priority support. Your dedicated technical partner — without the full-time cost. Good for startups and small teams without in-house developers.

consulting.title: Consulting & Review
consulting.desc: Architecture review, tech stack selection, MVP roadmapping, or a second opinion on your existing system. One-time engagement with a written report or call summary.

proof.label: PROOF OF WORK
proof.title: Built & used in production
proof.subtitle: These aren't demo projects. They're systems I designed, built, and run myself — evidence that the services above work in practice.

notecast.desc: A self-hosted note management system I built for myself and use daily. Demonstrates: JWT authentication, block-based rich text editing (EditorJS), third-party publishing APIs (WeChat, Feishu), AI integration (Claude), workspace management, and a full Spring Boot + Nuxt.js production stack. This portfolio website runs on it.

ims.desc: A full-lifecycle inventory system built for a real construction project. Demonstrates: complex multi-table relational data modeling, multi-level approval workflow design, role-based access control, Docker deployment, and a production-grade admin UI. Complete from requirements to deployment.

step1.title: Call & Scope
step1.desc: We discuss your project goals, challenges, and requirements to define the scope.

step2.title: Plan & Prototype
step2.desc: A detailed plan, timeline, and interactive prototypes are created for your review.

step3.title: Build & Test
step3.desc: Development begins with regular updates and rigorous testing to ensure quality.

step4.title: Launch & Improve
step4.desc: After a successful launch, we monitor performance and plan future enhancements.

fixed.title: Fixed-price for well-defined projects
fixed.desc: Best for projects with clear scope and deliverables. You get a fixed quote upfront with no surprises.

hourly.title: Hourly rate for small tasks & consulting
hourly.desc: Flexible option for smaller tasks, maintenance work, or consulting sessions. Pay only for the time spent.

retainer.title: Monthly retainer for long-term cooperation
retainer.desc: Ongoing support and development with predictable monthly costs. Priority support and dedicated availability.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about working with me

items.q: How long does a typical project take?
items.a: Project timelines vary based on scope and complexity. A simple website might take 2-4 weeks, while a full SaaS application can take 2-4 months. I provide detailed timeline estimates during our initial consultation.

items.q: Do you work with international clients?
items.a: Yes! I work with clients worldwide. I'm experienced in remote collaboration and can adapt to different time zones. All communication is handled via email, video calls, and project management tools.

items.q: What technologies do you specialize in?
items.a: I specialize in Vue.js, Nuxt.js, Node.js, and PHP for web development. For mobile apps, I use UniApp. I also have extensive experience with databases (MySQL, MongoDB), Docker, and cloud deployment.

items.q: Do you provide ongoing support after project completion?
items.a: Yes, I offer maintenance and support packages. This includes bug fixes, security updates, feature enhancements, and technical support. We can arrange monthly retainer agreements for ongoing needs.

For fixed-price projects, I typically require 30% upfront, 40% at midpoint, and 30% upon completion. For hourly or retainer work, billing is done monthly. All terms are clearly outlined in our contract.

items.q: Can you help with existing projects or only new ones?
items.a: I can definitely help with existing projects! Whether you need bug fixes, new features, performance optimization, or a complete refactor, I'm happy to jump in and contribute to your existing codebase.

items.q: Do you sign NDAs and work on confidential projects?
items.a: Absolutely. I understand the importance of confidentiality and am happy to sign NDAs before we begin discussing your project. Your intellectual property and business information are always kept secure.

items.q: What information do you need to provide a quote?
items.a: To provide an accurate quote, I need to understand your project requirements, target audience, desired features, timeline, and budget range. The more detailed your requirements, the more accurate my estimate will be.

cta.title: Have a Project in Mind?
cta.desc: Let's discuss your requirements and see how I can help you achieve your goals.

hero.title: Projects & Products
